## Veteran Community Partnerships

![Veteran Community Partnerships Header. Four diverse hands joined hand to wrist in a square with Veteran Community Partnerships spelled out on the right side.](/HEALTHPARTNERSHIPS/images/VCPheader.jpg)  

If you are looking for the Veterans Curation Program visit [veteranscurationprogram.org](https://www.veteranscurationprogram.org)

## About VCP

Veteran Community Partnerships (VCPs) are organized partnerships through which local VA facilities connect with state and local community services and agencies to:

* Develop and foster strong relationships
* Enhance and improve access to care, services, and benefits
* Promote seamless transitions
* Educate community agencies and VA providers
* Support caregivers and families
* Measure results through shared reporting

To find a VCP in your area, please contact the VCP Leadership Team at [VCP@va.gov](mailto:VCP@va.gov).

[![Veteran Community Partnerships Ask Campaign Banner. Rectangular banner that says, "Ask the question, did you or a loved one serve in the military? Explore VA benefits and services. Download VA's welcome kit."](/HEALTHPARTNERSHIPS/images/VCPaskcampaignbanner.png)](https://www.va.gov/welcome-kit/?utm_source=ask&utm_medium=ask&utm_id=ask)  
[Click here to learn more about ASK the Question](/HEALTHPARTNERSHIPS/vcpresources.asp)

**Why VCP?**

**VCP Expansion:**  
The Veteran Community Partnership initiative plans to [expand to every VA medical center by September 2024](https://www.va.gov/healthpartnerships/updates/vcp/01042021.asp). Guidance for VCP’s expansion and strategic plan is provided by subject matter experts from a dozen VHA National Centers and Offices along with ex-officio involvement from national non-VA organizations.

**How can my VA Medical Center develop a VCP?**

VCP trainings are available to assist VA facilities to develop a VCP within their communities.  To learn more about VCP and training opportunities, please contact VCP's Leadership Team at [VCP@va.gov](mailto:VCP@va.gov).

**External Link Disclaimer:** This page contains links that will take you outside of the Department of Veterans Affairs website. VA does not endorse and is not responsible for the content of the linked websites.

## About VCP

Veteran Community Partnerships (VCPs) are organized partnerships through which local VA facilities connect with state and local community services and agencies to:

To find a VCP in your area, please contact the VCP Leadership Team at [VCP@va.gov](mailto:VCP@va.gov).

![Veteran Community Partnerships Ask Campaign Banner. Rectangular banner that says, "Ask the question, did you or a loved one serve in the military? Explore VA benefits and services. Download VA's welcome kit."](/HEALTHPARTNERSHIPS/images/VCPaskcampaignbanner.png)

**Why VCP?**  
VCP provides an innovative, flexible, relevant and useful approach to establish and nurture community partnerships that facilitate and improve access to care, benefits and services from VA and community partners for Veterans, families and their caregivers.

**VCP Expansion:**  
The Veteran Community Partnership initiative plans to [expand to every VA medical center by September 2024](https://www.va.gov/healthpartnerships/updates/vcp/01042021.asp). Guidance for VCP’s expansion and strategic plan is provided by subject matter experts from a dozen VHA National Centers and Offices along with ex-officio involvement from national non-VA organizations.

**How can my VA Medical Center develop a VCP?**

VCP trainings are available to assist VA facilities to develop a VCP within their communities.  To learn more about VCP and training opportunities, please contact VCP's Leadership Team at [VCP@va.gov](mailto:VCP@va.gov).

--- SOURCE 4: Community Partners - We Honor Veterans ---
URL: https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/

SUMMARY:
We Honor Veterans, originally designed for hospice providers, is pleased to extend its partner opportunities to other community-based health care organizations.

FULL CONTENT:
![Alliance icon](/wp-content/themes/wehonorveterans/images/alliance-bb-community-mark.svg)

A program of the National Alliance for Care at Home

![We Honor Veterans](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/wp-content/uploads/whv-primary@3x.png)

We Honor Veterans

Caring Professionals on a Mission to Serve

#### [Community Partners](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/)

# Community Partners

We Honor Veterans, originally designed for hospice providers, is pleased to extend its partner opportunities to other community-based health care organizations providing direct care for seriously ill Veterans and their families.

WHV Founding Community Partners (Dignity Memorial) and Community Partners may include funeral homes, hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, assisted living facilities, home health agencies and other providers that understand the importance of serving Veterans based on their preferences for care and services.

The WHV Founding and Community Partner program has four levels of distinction based on organizations’ involvement and activities. The goal of accomplishing these levels is to provide the best quality of care to those who have served our country.

Becoming a WHV Community Partner will help ensure your organization is better prepared to:

To enroll as a WHV Founding Community Partner (Dignity Memorial) or Community Partner, a financial contribution is required. Please contact [veterans@allianceforcareathome.org](mailto:veterans@allianceforcareathome.org?subject=Become%20a%20WHV%20Community%20Partner) for more information.

Visit the [Founding Community **(Dignity Memorial)** and Community Partner Recruit page](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/recruit-requirements/) for additional details and to get started.

## Community Partner Levels

As a **We Honor Veterans**Founding Community Partner (Dignity Memorial) orCommunity Partner, there are four levels your location can achieve. Each level guides you through specific activities to progressively increase your ability to serve Veterans and their families. The primary goals of each Partner level are described below:

**[Recruit](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/recruit-requirements/)**: Get oriented and commit to the **We Honor Veterans** program

**[Level  One](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/level-one-requirements/):** Build professional and organizational capacity to provide quality services for Veterans and their families

**[Level Two](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/level-two-requirements/):** Develop and/or strengthen partnerships with hospices, VA, Veteran organizations and other healthcare organizations.

[**Level Three**](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/level-three-requirements/): Promote hospice and palliative care for Veterans.

**[Level Four](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/earn-your-stars/community-partners/level-four-requirement/):** Demonstrate the impact of organizational programs through evaluation and analysis of programs.

“Earn your stars” and matching logo by completing activities for each of the four levels of commitment. This enables Veterans, their families and community members to easily identify your commitment to offer Veteran-specific services provided by a competent and highly skilled workforce.

As a Founding Community Partner (Dignity Memorial) or Community Partner, you may promote your level of commitment to Veterans by displaying the **We Honor Veterans** Community Partner logo on your website, as well as community outreach and educational materials. As your location progresses through the four levels, you will also receive a personalized Certificate of Recognition to be proudly displayed in your facility.

## Footer

[![We Honor Veterans logo](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/whv_ribbon_white.png)](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/)

![We Honor Veterans logo](https://www.wehonorveterans.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/whv_ribbon_white.png)

A program of the [National Alliance for Care at Home](https://allianceforcareathome.org/)

SUMMARY:
Community partnerships are a powerful tool for enhancing the DAV's impact and ensuring that veterans receive the comprehensive support they need.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Collaborating with local community organizations enhances access to veteran services, providing tailored support and resources to improve healthcare outcomes for veterans and their families.
Forming alliances with private sector companies allows for innovative healthcare solutions, leveraging technology and resources to improve the quality and efficiency of veteran care.
Utilizing shared resources among VA and community partners facilitates comprehensive care, ensuring veterans receive holistic support through effective collaboration and resource optimization.
